criteria.criterion: Purposeful, consistent type scale and heading/body hierarchy — not default browser sizing.
Deliberate font-weight relationships; weight used to signal hierarchy, not at random.
Body copy is readable: sensible measure (~45-80ch), line-height, and size.
Color is tied to roles/purpose (semantic), not generic defaults; a coherent palette.
Avoids AI-default palettes (purple→blue gradient, lone acid accent, cream+terracotta).
Text and interactive elements meet WCAG AA contrast.
Consistent spacing scale / tokens; alignment and rhythm are intentional.
No gratuitous ornamentation (decorative side-stripes, superfluous borders).
Visual hierarchy matches task priority; the primary action is obvious.
No gradient-text, glassmorphism, or decorative motion used reflexively.
Does not read as templated / generated output; feels intentional and specific.
Copy is plain and specific; no hyperbolic marketing language.
Reuses component variants and applies the same rules across elements consistently.
Uses a coherent token system (color roles, shape, elevation) in the spirit of Material 3.
Interactive elements have clear state feedback (hover, press, focus, disabled).
Real semantic elements (button/a/nav/heading) rather than click-handlers on divs.
Inputs have associated labels; images have alt text; icon-only controls are labeled.
Visible keyboard focus states; tab order is sensible.
